Basic H2S.title: Safety Briefing - Basic H2S
discussion_points: The individual right of all those involved in the exercise to intervene and stop exercises if there is unsafe acts and conditions,
Engagement to prevent reoccurrence and the right to raise the alarm with staff if someone is thought to be in distress
Weather conditions of training area
Medical Provisions – Location of equipment
Medical Emergency Response Staff & telephone numbers
Verification & Confirmation of safety checks / pre-check lists / inspections / EBA pressure gauges indicator needle green zone (fully charged).
Exercises to be undertaken and their performance/ Assessment criteria
All jewelry, including but not limited to rings, bracelets, watches, necklaces, earrings, and piercings, must be removed before the commencement of any practical session.

practical_items.activity_detail:
Lifting EBA cylinders
Breathing from pressurized EBA cylinders
Connecting pressurized cascade cylinders
practical_items.risk_points:
Exercise Risk and corresponding emergency responses
1.Poor air quality/ Contaminated air
2.Lumbar strain while lifting fully charged EBA cylinders
3.BA Cylinder Explosion if over charged, dropped, not frequently maintained
4.Drop Cylinder on foot
5.Learners has right to stop the exercise if in case EBA cylinders not fully charged.

practical_items.activity_detail:
Mustering
Donning of lifejacket
Boarding (Please follow instructions on
boarding and where to sit)
Strapping In (Please ensure that when you
have boarded, take your seat as indicated
and fasten belt)
practical_items.risk_points:
TEMPSC
Injury when boarding
/disembarking due to tight
doorway / manoeuvring
during boarding
Uncontrolled release of
Lifeboat
Presence of FPD as a
secondary safety device to
avoid free fall

practical_items.activity_detail:
Individual:
Swimming
HELP
Wave Slap Protection
Group survival techniques
Towing
Chain
Huddle
Circle
The fitting of a helicopter lifting strop,
subsequent lifting and (simulated) entry
into a rescue helicopter including
boarding a marine liferaft from the water
and carrying out initial actions
practical_items.risk_points:
Sea Survival
Drowning (swimmers /
non-swimmers shall be
identified by helmet
colour)
Impact injury
Slip trip and fall injury
Rescue rings with rope
attached placed in centre
area of pool, close to
Pool Lifeguards.

practical_items.activity_detail:
Raising the alarm
Danger
Response the Instructor approaches the
casualty, goes down on one knee
Airway
Breathing - The Instructor then
demonstrates checking for breathing by
leaning forward over the casualty CPR
practical_items.risk_points:
Emergency First Aid
Cross contamination.
Injury when raising the
Alarm, Ergonomics, Back
injury, bending whilst
conducting CPR / Standing
from CPR position
Slip, trip fall or strain

practical_items.activity_detail:
Actions on discovering a fire
Raising the alarm
Class of Fire (A / B)
Correct selection of fire extinguisher
Water / Foam
CO2
Correct Operation and use of fire
extinguisher
practical_items.risk_points:
Fire Fighting
Close proximity to
demonstrations using live
class A and B materials
Real fires so main risk is
burns from fire
Potential risk is of fire
extinguisher failure (Hose
/ valve assembly failure
at thread / high pressure
release)
Impact injury

practical_items.activity_detail:
Donning of an aviation lifejacket
Evacuation from HUET into Liferaft
(Operating a push out window) - on water
surface
Submerge HUET
Capsize HUET
Inflate lifejacket, don spray hood, board
aviation liferaft
practical_items.risk_points:
HUET
Drowning (swimmers /
non-swimmers shall be
identified by RED helmet
colour)
Entrapment. Delegate fails
to exit HUET
Impact injury

practical_items.activity_detail:
Donning of an aviation lifejacket
Donning submersion / immersion suit
Donning of a rebreather EBS and practice
activation and usage at poolside.
Practice the use of the rebreather EBS
while standing on the guard rails in a
submerged position underwater, i.e.
experience of positive and negative
pressure by the learners.
Practice the use of the rebreather EBS
while moving across the width of the
swimming pool in a submerged body position
by using the guardrail as reference from
one end to the other.
Evacuation from HUET simulator on water
surface into
Heli raft / Life raft by Operating a push
out window
practical_items.risk_points:
HUET WITH REBREATHER EBS
Drowning (swimmers /
non-swimmers shall be
identified by YELLOW
helmet colour)
Entrapment. Delegate fails
to exit HUET
Impact injury
Sit and stretch one’s legs
while donning the survival
suits.
Identify broken neck and
wrist seals on the
survival suits prior to
the underwater exercises
and report it.
Nasal inhalation with EBS
prevented by using nose
clip

practical_items.activity_detail:
Donning of an aviation lifejacket
Donning submersion / immersion suit
Donning of a rebreather EBS and practice
activation and usage at poolside.
Practice the use of the rebreather EBS
while standing on the guard rails in a
submerged position underwater, i.e.
experience of positive and negative
pressure by the learners.
Practice the use of the rebreather EBS
while moving across the width of the
swimming pool in a submerged body position
by using the guardrail as reference from
one end to the other.
Evacuation from HUET simulator on water
surface into
Heliraft / Life raft by Operating a push
out window
Evacuate from partially submerged HUET
simulator with and without window using an
activated rebreather EBS
Evacuate the capsized HUET simulator with
or without window using an activated
rebreather EBS
Inflate lifejacket, don spray hood, board
aviation life raft.
practical_items.risk_points:
HUET WITH REBREATHER EBS
Drowning (swimmers /
non-swimmers shall be
identified by YELLOW
helmet colour)
Entrapment. Delegate fails
to exit HUET
Impact injury
Sit and stretch one’s legs
while donning the survival
suits.
Identify broken neck and
wrist seals on the
survival suits prior to
the underwater exercises
and report it.
Nasal inhalation with EBS
prevented by using nose
clip
If the EBS pouches are
ineffectively or
insufficiently secured
around the neck of the
learner it leads to
dislodgement causing
injuries and delays in
underwater evacuation.
On time activation of the
rebreather EBS ensures to
avoid water entry into the
counter lungs.
Unable to breathe from the
EBS due to its
dislodgement caused by an
automatic reflex in the

discussion_points: The individual right of all those involved in the exercise to intervene and stop exercises if there is unsafe acts and conditions,
Engagement to prevent reoccurrence and the right to raise the alarm with staff if someone is thought to be in distress
Conditions of training area (weather, temperature, noise)
Medical Provisions – Location of equipment
Medical Emergency Response Staff & telephone numbers

practical_items.activity_detail:
Donning of an aviation lifejacket
Donning submersion / immersion suit
Donning of a rebreather EBS and practice
activation and usage at poolside.
Practice the use of the rebreather EBS
while standing on the guard rails in a
submerged position underwater, i.e.
experience of positive and negative
pressure by the learners.
Practice the use of the rebreather EBS
while moving across the width of the
swimming pool in a submerged body
position by using the guardrail as
reference from one end to the other.
Evacuation from HUET simulator on water
surface into
Heliraft / Life raft by Operating a push
out window
Evacuate from partially submerged HUET
simulator with and without window using
an activated rebreather EBS

Inflate lifejacket, don spray hood,

practical_items.risk_points:
HUET WITH REBREATHER EBS
Drowning (swimmers / non
swimmers shall be
identified by YELLOW
helmet colour)
Entrapment. Delegate fails
to exit HUET
Impact injury
Sit and stretch one’s legs
while donning the survival
suits.
Identify broken neck and
wrist seals on the
survival suits prior to
the underwater exercises
and report it.
Nasal inhalation with EBS
prevented by using nose
clip
If the EBS pouches are
ineffectively or
insufficiently secured
around the neck of the
learner it leads to
dislodgement causing
injuries and delays in
underwater evacuation.
On time activation of the
rebreather EBS ensures to
avoid water entry into the
counter lungs.
Unable to breathe from the
EBS due to its
dislodgement caused by an
automatic reflex in the
body while water drops
from the counter lung
enter the airway.
EBS creates buoyancy when
inflated which increases
the effort required to
evacuate the simulator
while one’s underwater.
